prompt: Name one commonly-praised idea, trend, or practice in modern knowledge work that you think is overrated, and explain why.
response: One commonly-praised idea in modern knowledge work that I believe is overrated is the emphasis on constant multitasking. While it may seem efficient to juggle multiple tasks at once, research has shown that our brains are not truly capable of multitasking effectively. Instead, we rapidly switch between tasks, which can lead to decreased productivity, increased errors, and heightened stress levels. Focusing on one task at a time, or single-tasking, can often lead to better results and a more manageable workload.
